ops.title: release-app.yml rehearsal: green rc + stable, one workflow fix, two template gaps
ops.body:
Rehearsed yasyf/homebrew-tap `.github/workflows/release-app.yml@main` end-to-end on a private scratch consumer (yasyf/daemonkit-release-rehearsal), a minimal host-app-only XcodeGen project hand-rendered from daemonkit/templates. Both an rc tag (v0.0.1-rc.2) and a stable no-cask tag (v0.0.1) went fully green.

## Workflow fix landed in homebrew-tap (commit 4c1c4e3)
`release-app.yml` wrote its version xcconfig to RUNNER_TEMP only AFTER `xcodegen generate`, so a consumer project.yml wiring `Version.xcconfig` in via `configFiles` (daemonkit's template shape) hard-failed `xcodegen generate` spec validation ("Invalid config file Version.xcconfig"). Fix: compute the commit-count build number and write `$project_dir/Version.xcconfig` BEFORE `xcodegen generate`; point `version-xcconfig` output at it. Minimal, actionlint-green. This blocked EVERY real consumer, not just the minimal shape.

## Consumer-side gaps (for daemonkit templates / docs, NOT fixed by me)
1. A caller whose repo has default-read workflow token permissions must set `permissions: contents: write` on the calling job, or the run dies at `startup_failure` (a reusable workflow can't exceed the caller's token grant). daemonkit ships no caller-workflow template; the required permissions are undocumented. Worth a documented caller example.
2. The workflow does NOT run the consumer's `gen-version-xcconfig.sh`; it supplies versions via its own git-count `-xcconfig`. The template README's render order (gen-version-xcconfig.sh first) is not what CI does. CFBundleVersion in CI = `git rev-list --count HEAD` (epoch-nanos only for local dev).

## Evidence (rc.2, run 29626330905)
- Signed `Developer ID Application: Yasyf Mohamedali (SXKCTF23Q2)`, TeamIdentifier=SXKCTF23Q2, hardened runtime (flags 0x10000), secure timestamp; entitlements blob empty → no get-task-allow.
- Notarization `{"status":"Accepted","id":"16c93e68-..."}`; stapler validate OK; spctl "accepted, source=Notarized Developer ID".
- rc tag skipped the cask job and registry guard (stable=false); release marked prerelease; homebrew-tap main unchanged.
- Artifact = full universal (x86_64+arm64) `.app` bundle.

Stable v0.0.1 (run 29626528016): green, prerelease=false, cask still skipped (no cask_token), tap untouched. The cask PUBLISH path stays unproven (can't test without publishing a real cask).

Go/no-go for a pinned v1: GO once 4c1c4e3 is on the v1 tag.
